mnemonics.title: Tree Traversal Order
mnemonics.content: PLease Learn InOrder: Preorder (Root-Left-Right), Level order (Breadth), Inorder (Left-Root-Right), Postorder (Left-Right-Root) - Remember as 'P L I P' or think of PLI as 'Please'.

mnemonics.title: Binary Tree Properties
mnemonics.content: N = Nodes, E = Edges, L = Leaves, I = Internal nodes. Remember: N = E + 1, L = I + 1 (for non-empty binary trees). Think 'Nodes have One more Edge'.

mnemonics.title: BST Insertion
mnemonics.content: Smaller goes LEFT (Less = Left), Greater goes RIGHT. Remember: 'L < R' - Left is Less, Right is bigger.

acronyms.usage: Used for efficient searching, insertion, deletion in O(log n) time when balanced.
Self-balancing BST where heights of left and right subtrees differ by at most 1.
Traversal technique exploring as deep as possible before backtracking.
Level order traversal - explores all nodes at current depth before moving deeper.

visualTips.visualization: Think of an organizational chart or family tree. Root at top, children below, like an upside-down tree growing downwards.
visualTips.usage: Level 1 = Root, Level 2 = Children of root, etc.

visualTips.visualization: Draw each node as a circle with two empty slots below it (left and right). Fill slots based on insertion order.
visualTips.usage: Helps visualize parent-child relationships and understand traversals.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a number line. Left subtree = numbers to the left (smaller), right subtree = numbers to the right (larger).
visualTips.usage: Inorder traversal gives sorted sequence because it visits left (smaller), then root, then right (larger).

analogies.analogy: Like the CEO of a company - topmost position, everyone reports to someone below them eventually.
Like employees with no subordinates - they do the actual work with no one reporting to them.
Like a family tree - parents are above children, grandchildren are below, siblings share same parents.
Like counting floors in a building - ground floor is level 1, height is number of floors minus one in edges, or total floors in levels.
Like a single file line - each person points to only one person ahead, like a linked list disguised as a tree.
